codeReview.summary: The feature mostly wires socketing UI and stat propagation correctly, but it does not fully satisfy the gem spec because higher-tier Diamond gems were not implemented and the shipped tests/handoff overstate gem stack sizes relative to the actual data.

issues.description: Only one Diamond gem (`g_diamond_1`) exists, so the implementation does not meet the requirement that higher tiers provide larger bonuses for all gem types. Ruby/Sapphire/Emerald/Topaz have T1-T3, but Diamond lacks T2/T3 definitions and corresponding stat mappings.

The test asserts gem `maxStack` is 10, while the feature description/handoff claimed gems are stackable to 20. This mismatch means the review transcript and tests overstate the implemented behavior, and the skill's prescribed test coverage did not catch the discrepancy between spec and code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `GameEvents.GEM_SOCKET_OPEN` was added but is never emitted or consumed; the socket panel is opened directly from `UIScene` inventory slot handlers instead. AGENTS.md documents checking EventBus payload contracts, but there is no shared guidance about avoiding dead event additions for UI features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/utils/EventBus.ts:50 adds `GEM_SOCKET_OPEN`; repo search shows no other references beyond that declaration.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The systems-worker skill says to write tests first and cover expected behavior, but the transcript shows implementation landed before the gem tests, and the resulting tests/handoff still contain a spec mismatch around gem stack size and supported gem tiers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session deb5674f-bce1-45f2-a479-a2953212e930 describes code edits before `src/__tests__/GemSocketing.test.ts` creation; compare handoff claim of higher-tier support to src/data/items/bases.ts where only `g_diamond_1` exists and src/__tests__/GemSocketing.test.ts:121-125 asserting maxStack 10.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture library notes a combat/stats pipeline caveat, but it does not yet capture the specific gem/allStats behavior: `InventorySystem.getEquipmentStats()` expands `allStats` into six primaries before `ZoneScene` caches typed equip stats and `Player.recalcDerived()` consumes them. This was central to the worker's reasoning and would be useful shared knowledge for future stat features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/systems/InventorySystem.ts:147-153 expands `allStats`; src/scenes/ZoneScene.ts:1791-1809 caches typed equip stats; src/entities/Player.ts:106-131 applies primary stat bonuses in `recalcDerived()`.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, skill instructions, and shared-state docs. The feature correctly adds socket UI, insert/remove flows, tooltip/character-panel updates, and stat propagation, but it fails the written spec because Diamond gems do not have higher tiers with larger bonuses like the other gem families. Also noted a dead `GEM_SOCKET_OPEN` event and a test/spec mismatch around gem stack size.
